comments_critique.summary: The discussion centers on the security implications of using ECC versus RSA in the context of quantum computing threats. One commenter expresses skepticism about ECC's vulnerability to quantum attacks and questions the trade-offs made for fitting cryptographic schemes into UDP constraints.

comments.author: bob1029
comments.text:
> ECC algorithms with smaller key sizes would be more vulnerable to a quantum attack, as it would require a currently theoretical quantum computer with fewer qubits than would be required for an RSA key with the same cryptographic strength [25].

This is what keeps me skeptical about ECC. RSA is really chunky, and maybe that's a fundamental advantage from an information theory perspective. Compromising on the crypto scheme because we can't fit inside UDP seems like a cursed path.

[25]: https://arxiv.org/abs/1706.06752
comments.analysis: The comment accurately references a known concern in cryptography regarding ECC's vulnerability to quantum attacks compared to RSA, supported by a relevant academic source, making it a well-informed and constructive contribution.
